body:
Follow-up from PR #60 review: the current generated-comment minimization code works for the handoff cleanup change, but the helper API has grown around review-specific naming and one-line wrappers.

The goal is to redesign this area systematically rather than making PR #60 larger.

Proposed direction:

- Create one clearer generic path for collapsing generated comments/reviews.
- Model target sources explicitly: issue comments, PR comments, and PR reviews.
- Keep behavior-specific policies separate for AI review synthesis, rubrics reviews, and orchestrator handoff markers.
- Remove wrappers that only forward to a private function without adding policy or meaning.
- Preserve existing behavior and test coverage while making the API easier to extend.

Context:

- PR: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/60
- Review discussion: the exported handoff cleanup wrapper is mostly stylistic/redundant, but a broader cleanup is better suited to a separate PR.

comments.body: @sepo-agent /answer it seems it is also connected to the following two issues #67 #68

Yes, I agree they are connected.

I’d treat #65 as the umbrella/refactor issue, with #67 and #68 as concrete behavior cases that the redesign should support:

- #67: extend review cleanup beyond generated review synthesis to older inline/generated review comments when a fresh review is posted.
- #68: add the same “latest generated status stays visible, older ones collapse” behavior for repeated `/fix-pr` result comments.
- #65: redesign the shared helper/API so these are policies over one generic minimization path, instead of adding more one-off wrappers.

The current code already points in that direction: [`review-summary-minimize.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/review-summary-minimize.ts) mixes PR comments, PR reviews, issue comments, and policy-specific wrappers, while [`post-comment.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/post-comment.ts#L78-L92) only collapses old review synthesis comments for `route === "review"` and does not yet cover `/fix-pr` comments.

Suggested handling:

1. Keep #65 open as the design/refactor umbrella.
2. Link #67 and #68 from #65 as acceptance cases.
3. Implement #65 with explicit target/source types: issue comments, PR comments, PR reviews, and inline review comments if GitHub’s API supports the needed minimization target cleanly.
4. Add policy filters for review synthesis, rubrics reviews, orchestrator handoffs, fix-pr result comments, and inline review comments.
5. Close #67/#68 when the new shared path wires those behaviors and has focused regression tests.

title: Refactor generated comment minimization helpers
